name: Command Query Separation
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: A function both changes state (command) and returns a value (query), so callers cannot tell from a call site whether the function is asking a question or issuing an order, leading to confusing conditional expressions.
steps: Identify functions that both mutate state and return a value.
Split each such function into a command (void, mutates state) and a separate query (returns value, no mutation).
Name commands as imperative verbs (setAttribute) and queries as noun-phrases or questions (attributeExists, getAttribute).
Never use command return values as conditional predicates; query first, then command.
Apply this principle at every layer: service, repository, and domain.
